messages.content:
An air purifier manufacturer is looking for ideas on using edge computing to enhance its products and services.

Which approach would make use of edge computing technologies?


a local support staff based in the customer's geographic location for more rapid responses


a device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed


a scheduling feature that activates a customer's air purifier at a defined time each day


an automated program that can interpret customer service requests and assign agents accordingly


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

a device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed

This approach utilizes edge computing as the device in the customer's home is equipped with sensors to detect air quality levels and process the data locally. It can then send alerts or notifications to the user based on the analysis of the data, without needing to send the data to a centralized server for processing.

What describes the relationship between 5G and edge computing?


Edge is a new network that competes with 5G.


5G expands the possibilities of edge computing.


5G networks will make edge computing obsolete.


Edge computing is not possible without 5G.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

5G expands the possibilities of edge computing.

5G networks provide faster and more reliable connectivity, which enables edge computing to be even more powerful and efficient. Edge computing leverages the capabilities of 5G networks to process and analyze data locally at the edge of the network, reducing latency and improving real-time decision-making capabilities. Therefore, 5G and edge computing are complementary technologies that work together to enhance various applications and services.

If a person's mobile phone was designed to work with 4G technology, what will happen when a 5G network is rolled out in their area?


Their phone will automatically upgrade itself to work with the new 5G network.


They will lose service as the 4G network is deactivated to make room for the 5G network.


They will still have service through the 4G network for at least several years.


Their phone will only work when they are out of range of any 5G networks.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

They will still have service through the 4G network for at least several years.

When a new 5G network is rolled out in an area, existing 4G networks do not immediately get deactivated. Mobile phones designed to work with 4G technology will continue to have service through the 4G network even when 5G is available. It typically takes time for 5G networks to become widespread and for older network technologies like 4G to be phased out. Therefore, users with 4G phones will still be able to use their devices until they choose to upgrade to a 5G-compatible phone.

A frozen-food factory needs to monitor temperatures on machines, check inventory of supplies, and provide sensors on lights and doors.

What should the factory do to address these needs?


use more cell sites to better record daily activity


improve the latency over the wireless network


install devices that utilize edge computing


add antennas inside and outside the building


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

install devices that utilize edge computing

To address the needs of monitoring temperatures on machines, checking inventory of supplies, and providing sensors on lights and doors, the factory should install devices that utilize edge computing. Edge computing involves placing computing and processing capabilities closer to the source of data generation, such as the machines, sensors, and devices in this case. By using edge computing devices, the factory can collect and analyze data locally, reducing latency and improving real-time decision-making capabilities. This allows for timely monitoring of temperatures, inventory management, and sensor readings without the need for centralized processing or reliance on the cloud.

A retailer is able to track which products draw the most attention from its customers through the use of 5G-enabled motion sensors placed around the store. With this knowledge, the retailer can supply the proper amount of products to meet customer demand.

Which technology combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way?


internet of things


beamforming


platooning


network slicing


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

internet of things

The technology that combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way is the Internet of Things (IoT). The IoT refers to a network of interconnected devices that can communicate and exchange data with each other. In this scenario, the motion sensors placed around the store would be considered IoT devices that can detect and track customer movements and interactions with products. By leveraging 5G connectivity, these motion sensors can transmit data in real-time, enabling the retailer to monitor shopping trends and adjust their product supply accordingly.

Why does 5G mmWave require more cells to achieve a better signal?


Higher bandwidth requires more transmitters.


Lower bandwidth requires consistent coverage.


Lower frequencies are more easily blocked.


Higher frequencies have a shorter range.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

Higher frequencies have a shorter range.

5G mmWave operates at higher frequencies, which have shorter wavelengths and higher data transfer rates. However, these frequencies also have a shorter range compared to lower frequencies used in other wireless technologies. Higher frequencies tend to be more easily obstructed by obstacles such as buildings, trees, and even weather conditions like rain or fog. To overcome these challenges and achieve better signal strength, 5G mmWave requires more cells to be deployed in closer proximity. This dense cell deployment helps to mitigate the shorter range and ensure consistent coverage and improved signal quality in areas where 5G mmWave is available.

What underlying concept is edge computing based on?


delivering files to end users at the fastest possible speeds


using beamforming to improve the efficiency of a network


moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data


transmitting the maximum amount of data over a single connection


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data

Edge computing is based on the concept of moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data. It involves placing computational power and data storage capabilities at the edge of the network, closer to where the data is generated or consumed. This approach reduces latency by processing and analyzing data locally, rather than sending it to a centralized cloud or data center for processing. By moving computing resources closer to the source or user, edge computing enables faster real-time processing, improves response times, and reduces the dependency on the network for data processing and analysis.

What is a benefit of 5G mmWave technology?


It easily passes through obstacles such as walls and trees.


It has low frequency, allowing it to transmit over longer ranges.


It can transmit a large amount of information with low latency.


It provides coverage to low-density rural and suburban areas.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

It can transmit a large amount of information with low latency.

One of the benefits of 5G mmWave technology is its ability to transmit a large amount of information with low latency. The mmWave spectrum used in 5G offers wide bandwidth, which allows for faster data transfer speeds and increased capacity for transmitting large amounts of data. Additionally, mmWave can support low latency, enabling real-time applications and services that require instant responsiveness, such as autonomous vehicles, remote surgery, or augmented reality. However, it is worth noting that the mmWave frequency range has shorter range and is more susceptible to obstruction, which can affect coverage in certain scenarios.

Which situation would benefit the most by using edge computing?


an IT department wants to increase the capacity of its central server


an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data


a clothing company wants to track customer preferences


a theater wants to record performances for its website


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data

An offshore oil rig needing to more efficiently process data would benefit the most from edge computing. In this scenario, the oil rig may have limited or intermittent connectivity to a centralized server or cloud infrastructure due to its remote location. By implementing edge computing on the rig, the data processing and analysis can be performed locally, closer to the source of data generation. This reduces the reliance on the network connection, minimizes latency, and allows for real-time decision-making and monitoring. Edge computing enables efficient processing of critical data on-site, enhancing operational efficiency and safety in environments where reliable connectivity is a challenge.

A large manufacturing company is looking to optimize worker safety on the shop floor.

What is a way the company can utilize edge computing to help achieve this?


Require supervisors to verify workers are wearing the correct protective gear before they can enter the shop floor.


Provide safety training videos at workstations on the shop floor to ensure proper machine operation by workers.


Deploy robots that move across the shop floor and clean the work area before new workers can enter the workspace.


Use a central unit to log workers' current safety equipment and process what equipment is needed before future jobs.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

Use a central unit to log workers' current safety equipment and process what equipment is needed before future jobs.

Utilizing edge computing, the manufacturing company can use a central unit to log workers' current safety equipment and process what equipment is needed before future jobs. This central unit can be at the edge of the network and connected to sensors or devices that track the safety gear worn by workers. It can analyze the data in real-time and identify if workers are wearing the correct protective gear or if any equipment is missing. By doing so, the company can ensure worker safety by proactively identifying and addressing any gaps in safety equipment before workers enter the shop floor.

How does 5G technology enhance the Internet of Things (IoT)?


by installing larger cell towers to project stronger signals


by connecting remote devices through physical cables


by supporting greater numbers of connected devices


by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

by supporting greater numbers of connected devices

5G technology enhances the Internet of Things (IoT) by supporting greater numbers of connected devices. IoT devices are interconnected devices that communicate and exchange data with each other, enabling the sharing of information and automation of processes. 5G networks have the capability to handle a significantly larger number of connected devices compared to previous generations of wireless technology. This increased capacity allows for the efficient and seamless connectivity of a wide range of IoT devices, making it easier to deploy and manage large-scale IoT deployments. The enhanced capacity of 5G networks ensures that the IoT ecosystem can expand and evolve to accommodate the growing number of connected devices and the data they generate.

An electronics company develops a line of augmented reality headsets for use in interactive 3D media applications using 5G mmWave technology. However, customers in rural areas have been complaining about issues with an inability to connect more than their urban and suburban counterparts.

What could be contributing to the connectivity issues encountered by the rural customers?


The rural areas do not have enough cell locations to support mmWave.


The connection density in the rural areas is too high, causing reduced bandwidth.


The signals from the headsets are being downgraded to an earlier generation.


The cell towers in the rural areas are not large enough to support mmWave.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

The rural areas do not have enough cell locations to support mmWave.

Connectivity issues encountered by rural customers in this scenario could be due to a lack of sufficient cell locations to support mmWave technology. 5G mmWave operates at higher frequencies that have shorter wavelengths and limited range compared to lower frequency bands. As a result, mmWave signals are more easily obstructed by physical obstacles like buildings, trees, and even atmospheric conditions. Therefore, to provide reliable mmWave coverage in rural areas, a higher density of cell locations is required compared to urban or suburban areas. If the rural areas don't have enough cell locations to support mmWave, it can lead to connectivity issues and reduced signal reliability for customers in those areas.

What is a distinguishing feature of 5G mmWave?


It can easily penetrate walls and barriers.


It can transfer large amounts of data quickly.


It can support an infinite number of devices.


It has better reception for indoor use.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

It can transfer large amounts of data quickly.

A distinguishing feature of 5G mmWave technology is its ability to transfer large amounts of data quickly. MMWave operates within higher frequency bands, which allows for wider bandwidth and faster data transfer rates. This enables 5G mmWave to support high-bandwidth applications, such as ultra-HD video streaming, virtual reality, and augmented reality, where significant amounts of data need to be transmitted quickly and in real-time. While there are other advantages of 5G mmWave, such as low latency and high capacity, its ability to transfer large amounts of data quickly is a key characteristic that sets it apart from lower frequency bands used in other wireless technologies.

What does the Internet of Things (IoT) enable?


It controls network traffic in highly populated areas.


It increases the transmission range of network signals.


It translates signals between different network generations.


It allows everyday physical devices to exchange data.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

It allows everyday physical devices to exchange data.

The Internet of Things (IoT) enables everyday physical devices to exchange data. It involves connecting various devices, sensors, and objects to the internet and enabling them to communicate and share information with each other without human intervention. These devices can range from simple household appliances to complex industrial machinery. By connecting these devices, IoT enables data collection, analysis, and automation, leading to improved efficiency, convenience, and new opportunities for innovation across various domains such as smart homes, healthcare, transportation, and manufacturing.

What is Accenture's most important advantage when it comes to 5G and edge computing?


the amount of funds we have invested in cutting-edge technology


our extensive experience in dealing with disruptive technologies


the number of custom-built cell sites we have implemented


our market share in the fields of cell networks and computing


I don't know this yet.

I apologize, but as an AI language model, I don't have access to current market information or specific company details. Therefore, I cannot provide the most up-to-date information about Accenture's advantages in relation to 5G and edge computing. It would be best to refer to official Accenture resources, company announcements, or industry reports for the most accurate and recent information on Accenture's advantages in this context.

How does using myNav allow Accenture to help its clients become more sustainable?


by reorganizing manufacturing facilities to easily calculate carbon emissions


by facilitating design of cloud solutions that reduce carbon emissions


by creating new company initiatives to reduce transportation costs


by devising new regulations for disposing of outdated technology


I don't know this yet.

I'm sorry, but I'm not familiar with a specific product called "myNav" related to Accenture. It's possible that it may be an internal tool or platform developed by Accenture for specific purposes. Without more information about myNav, I am unable to provide a specific answer on how it might help Accenture clients become more sustainable. It would be best to consult official Accenture resources or reach out to the company directly to get the accurate information on how myNav relates to sustainability initiatives.

What is an example of new technology having a NEGATIVE impact on sustainability?


using smart meters to track energy consumption in homes


using multiple servers to maintain a cryptocurrency operation


using automation to shut down unused electric devices


using the cloud to analyze environmental data on a global scale


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

using multiple servers to maintain a cryptocurrency operation

An example of new technology having a negative impact on sustainability is using multiple servers to maintain a cryptocurrency operation. Cryptocurrency mining and transactions often require significant computational power and energy consumption. To support these operations, multiple servers or powerful computer systems are used, resulting in high energy consumption and carbon emissions. As a result, the increased demand for cryptocurrencies can contribute to environmental concerns and sustainability challenges. It's worth noting that sustainability impacts can also be mitigated by utilizing renewable energy sources and employing energy-efficient practices in such operations.

A manufacturing company has decided to be carbon free by 2030.

What can it do to achieve this goal?


Capture and dispose of atmospheric carbon dioxide.


Switch to power generated entirely by renewable sources.


Buy carbon credits to support reforestation projects.


Stop manufacturing any product that contains carbon.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

Switch to power generated entirely by renewable sources.

To achieve the goal of becoming carbon-free by 2030, the manufacturing company can switch to power generated entirely by renewable sources. Renewable energy sources such as solar, wind, hydro, or geothermal power do not produce carbon emissions when generating electricity. By transitioning their energy sources to renewables, the company can greatly reduce or eliminate the carbon footprint associated with its operations. This action aligns with the goal of reducing greenhouse gas emissions and promoting sustainability. It's important to note that other actions mentioned, such as capturing carbon dioxide or supporting reforestation projects through carbon credits, can also contribute to carbon neutrality. However, the switch to renewable energy is a fundamental step towards achieving carbon-free operations.

What is a benefit of a company publishing a sustainability report?


to describe its overall goals for economic growth


to publicize local environmental regulations


to provide information on its financial stability


to describe its environmental goals and progress


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

to describe its environmental goals and progress

A benefit of a company publishing a sustainability report is to describe its environmental goals and progress. Sustainability reports provide detailed information on a company's environmental performance and initiatives, outlining its goals, strategies, and progress in areas such as reducing carbon emissions, water conservation, waste management, and other environmental impacts. By publishing sustainability reports, companies can transparently communicate their environmental commitments and efforts to stakeholders, including customers, investors, employees, and the general public. These reports demonstrate accountability, foster trust, and allow stakeholders to assess the company's environmental performance and its progress towards sustainability goals.

What is an example of a way a farm could apply new technology to improve sustainability?


Purchase new farm equipment using blockchain-based cryptocurrency.


Use artificial intelligence to predict and adjust the farm's water usage.


Use machine learning to improve product transportation and delivery.


Build a data center on the farm premises to store more information.


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is:

Use artificial intelligence to predict and adjust the farm's water usage.

An example of how a farm could apply new technology to improve sustainability is by using artificial intelligence (AI) to predict and adjust the farm's water usage. AI can analyze a combination of data sources such as weather patterns, soil moisture levels, crop types, and historical data to optimize irrigation systems. By using AI algorithms, farmers can make informed decisions about when and how much water to apply, reducing water waste and improving water-use efficiency. This technology helps conserve water resources, lowers environmental impacts, and promotes sustainable farming practices.
